root_text.an7.62:1.3: Abhijānāmi kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, dīgharattaṁ katānaṁ puññānaṁ dīgharattaṁ iṭṭhaṁ kantaṁ manāpaṁ vipākaṁ paccanubhūtaṁ.
root_text.an7.62:1.5: Satta vassāni mettacittaṁ bhāvetvā satta saṁvaṭṭavivaṭṭakappe nayimaṁ lokaṁ punāgamāsiṁ.
root_text.an7.62:1.6: Saṁvaṭṭamāne sudāhaṁ, bhikkhave, loke ābhassarūpago homi,
root_text.an7.62:2.1: Tatra sudaṁ, bhikkhave, brahmā homi mahābrahmā abhibhū anabhibhūto aññadatthudaso vasavattī.
root_text.an7.62:2.2: Chattiṁsakkhattuṁ kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sakko ahosiṁ devānamindo;
root_text.an7.62:2.3: anekasatakkhattuṁ rājā ahosiṁ cakkavattī dhammiko dhammarājā cāturanto vijitāvī janapadatthāvariyappatto sattaratanasamannāgato.
root_text.an7.62:2.4: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhave, imāni satta ratanāni ahesuṁ, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.an7.62:2.5: cakkaratanaṁ, hatthiratanaṁ, assaratanaṁ, maṇiratanaṁ, itthiratanaṁ, gahapatiratanaṁ, pariṇāyakaratanameva sattamaṁ.
root_text.an7.62:2.6: Parosahassaṁ kho pana me, bhikkhave, puttā ahesuṁ sūrā vīraṅgarūpā parasenappamaddanā.
root_text.an7.62:2.7: So imaṁ pathaviṁ sāgarapariyantaṁ adaṇḍena asatthena dhammena abhivijiya ajjhāvasinti.

translation_text.an7.62:1.2: For ‘good deeds’ is a term for happiness.
translation_text.an7.62:1.3: I recall undergoing for a long time the likable, desirable, and agreeable results of good deeds performed over a long time.
translation_text.an7.62:1.4: I developed a mind of love for seven years.
translation_text.an7.62:1.5: As a result, for seven eons of the cosmos contracting and expanding I didn’t return to this world again.
translation_text.an7.62:1.6: As the cosmos contracted I went to the realm of streaming radiance.
translation_text.an7.62:1.7: As it expanded I was reborn in an empty mansion of divinity.
translation_text.an7.62:2.1: There I was the Divinity, the Great Divinity, the vanquisher, the unvanquished, the universal seer, the wielder of power.
translation_text.an7.62:2.2: I was Sakka, lord of gods, thirty-six times.
translation_text.an7.62:2.3: Many hundreds of times I was a king, a wheel-turning monarch, a just and principled king. My dominion extended to all four sides, I achieved stability in the country, and I possessed the seven treasures.
translation_text.an7.62:2.5: the wheel, the elephant, the horse, the jewel, the woman, the householder, and the commander as the seventh treasure.
translation_text.an7.62:2.6: I had over a thousand sons who were valiant and heroic, crushing the armies of my enemies.
translation_text.an7.62:2.7: After conquering this land girt by sea, I reigned by principle, without rod or sword.
translation_text.an7.62:3.1: See the result of good deeds,
translation_text.an7.62:3.2: of skillful deeds, for one seeking happiness.
translation_text.an7.62:3.3: I developed a mind of love
translation_text.an7.62:3.5: For seven eons of expansion and contraction
translation_text.an7.62:3.6: I didn’t return to this world again.
translation_text.an7.62:4.2: I went to the realm of streaming radiance.
translation_text.an7.62:4.4: I went to an empty mansion of divinity.
translation_text.an7.62:5.1: Seven times I was a Great Divinity,
translation_text.an7.62:5.2: and at that time I was the wielder of power.
translation_text.an7.62:5.3: Thirty-six times I was lord of gods,
translation_text.an7.62:5.4: acting as ruler of the gods.
translation_text.an7.62:6.1: Then I was king, a wheel-turning monarch,
translation_text.an7.62:6.2: lord of the Black Plum Tree Land.
translation_text.an7.62:6.4: I was sovereign of all humans.
translation_text.an7.62:8.3: I was born in a rich family,
translation_text.an7.62:9.1: It was replete with all sense pleasures,
translation_text.an7.62:9.3: This was well taught by the Buddhas,
translation_text.an7.62:10.1: This is the cause of greatness
translation_text.an7.62:10.2: by which one is called a lord of the land.
translation_text.an7.62:10.4: with lots of property and assets.
translation_text.an7.62:11.2: lord of the Black Plum Tree Land.
translation_text.an7.62:11.3: Who would not be inspired by this,
translation_text.an7.62:12.4: remembering the instructions of the Buddhas.”

variant_text.an7.62:1.2: yadidaṁ puññāni → yadidaṁ puññanti (bj); yadidaṁ puññānanti (sya-all, pts1ed, csp1ed)
variant_text.an7.62:1.3: bhikkhave, dīgharattaṁ katānaṁ puññānaṁ dīgharattaṁ iṭṭhaṁ → bhikkhave dīgharattaṁ iṭṭhaṁ (sya1ed, sya2ed), bhikkhave dīgharattaṁ puññānaṁ iṭṭhaṁ (?)
variant_text.an7.62:2.7: ajjhāvasinti → ajjhāvasati (si, mr); ajjhāvasanti (cck, sya1ed, pts1ed)
variant_text.an7.62:10.2: pathabyo me na vipajjati → puthavyo yena vuccati (bj, sya-all, pts1ed)
variant_text.an7.62:12.1: attakāmena → atthakāmena (bj, cck, sya1ed, pts1ed, mr)

reference_text.an7.62:1.1: csp1ed16.465, csp2ed16.465, dr23.99, ms16A7_401, msdiv62, pts-vp-pli4.89
